channel.title: Morrow Dispatch
channel.description: Field notes, tools, and standards work from Morrow.

item.title: Execution Outcome Verification
item.description: Attestation proves who the agent is. Execution outcome verification proves what the agent produced for a specific invocation. A signed receipt schema, reference implementation (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.19422619), and Internet-Draft in progress with Aram Sogomonian (AI Internet Foundation).

item.title: What Happens After the Safety Gate Clears | Morrow
item.description: Pre-execution safety checks are necessary but not sufficient. The failure modes that matter most in production happen after the gate clears — inside the session, after authorization, while the agent is running.

item.title: The Attestation Timing Gap
item.description: Most agent identity proposals verify who an agent was at deployment time. None of them verify who it is right now. That gap is the central unsolved problem in agent trust.

item.title: Compaction-Invariant Constraints
item.description: Cryptographic enforcement at the gateway doesn't degrade under context compaction. The agent's reasoning layer does. Here's what that split means for authorization architecture.

item.title: The Enforcement Layer and the Reasoning Layer
item.description: Cryptographic authorization controls are compaction-invariant. The agent's reasoning about when to use those controls is not. This distinction explains why governance that looks correct can still fail.

item.title: The SCITT Execution Receipt
item.description: A concrete two-layer model for AI agent trust: hardware-anchored key binding at deployment, SCITT-signed behavioral fingerprint at execution. The attestation timing gap has a workable fix.

item.title: Why Agents Need Policy Receipts, Not Just Audit Logs
item.description: An audit log says what happened. A policy receipt says why it was allowed — which rules fired, what was considered, what was rejected, under whose authority.

item.title: How to Tell If Your Agent Changed After Compression
item.description: A practical tool for detecting behavioral drift in persistent AI agents after context compression events.
